item.title: Why is this so hard to understand?
item.description: https://apple.news/ASOTal6sGTCyzM2kuHDHhPQ Dear Democrat Party, The people don’t want moderates. The people don’t want status quo. The people don’t want a return to “normal”. The people want you to admit the system is broken and fix it! We have a dying, decrepit system in the US that we’re tired of. Greedflation, stagnant min wage, horrible medical insurance, housing shortages…all of it. Trump didn’t start any of that, he’s just a robber baron swooping in to take advantage of the corpse lying on the side of the road. Hell, the reason Trump won over so many new voters this time was a promise of making our lives better. That people actually believed him is on them, but change is the populist platform. We (of the non MAGA crowd) want change too, we just want those things done constructively to benefit the people and not destructively to benefit Trump and his cronies. In short, dear Democrat Party, please stop trying to be the sane parent that’s holding the marriage together and be the parent who will do whatever it takes to protect the kids from the crazy, abusive parent.
item.dc:creator: Kammel

item.title: Bloomberg: "for about two years, employees at Evraz North America Inc., a Russian-owned steel plant operator in the US, falsified quality control test results on some armored plating for the vehicle’s manufacturer, according to an internal report"
item.description: "The incidents took place at an Evraz facility in Oregon from 2017 to 2019, according to Evraz’s probe and company officials, who said employees at the Portland plant skipped mandatory hardness tests and inputted fake results on an estimated 12,800 armor plates of various types. Those plates were labeled as tested and approved, though some later showed signs of cracking." "A primary customer for the armor plates was Oshkosh Defense LLC, a maker of a wide range of military vehicles, including the JLTV" https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-03-11/armor-plates-for-us-army-s-jltv-didn-t-pass-quality-control-tests
item.dc:creator: vladrichdemaclant

item.title: Turmeric ginger chicken soup recipe!
item.description: https://www.triedandtruerecipe.com/turmeric-ginger-chicken-soup/ Chicken broth: 3 pound chicken (whole or cut-up) 2 tablespoons neutral oil (divided) 1 yellow onion (peeled and cut into wedges) 6-inch piece fresh ginger (scrubbed and thinly sliced into rounds) 1 tablespoon turmeric powder 2 bay leaves 5 cloves garlic (peeled and lightly crushed) 1 tablespoon ground coriander 1 tablespoon black peppercorns Soup: 8 ounces shiitake mushrooms (stems discarded, caps thinly sliced) 1 yellow onion (peeled, halved, and thinly sliced into half-moons) 5 scallions (trimmed and minced; a pinch reserved for garnish) 3 medium carrots (peeled and thinly sliced on an angle) For serving: 20 ounces fresh ramen noodles (or rice noodles, soba, or udon) Chili oil Prepare the broth: Heat 1 tablespoon neutral oil in a 4-quart pot over medium heat until hot. Add the onion wedges, ginger slices, turmeric, bay leaves, garlic, coriander, and black peppercorns. Sauté for 2 to 3 minutes until fragrant. Place the chicken on top and season with 3 teaspoons kosher salt. Cover the chicken with 8 cups of water. Bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to low and cover the pot. Simmer for 1 hour or until the thickest part of the chicken breast reaches 165ºF. Shred the chicken: Remove the chicken from the pot and transfer it to a large bowl. Once the chicken is cool enough to handle, pick the meat from the bones and shred it with your fingers. Reserve the carcass for stock, if you like. Strain the broth: Strain the broth into a large bowl through a fine-mesh sieve. Discard the spent aromatics. Finish the soup: Wipe out the pot and heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of neutral oil over medium heat. Add the sliced shiitake mushrooms and sauté until browned, about 5 minutes. Add the minced green onions, thinly sliced onions, and carrots. Sauté for an additional 3 minutes. Add the shredded chicken and cook for 1 minute. Pour the reserved broth into the pot and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes or until the carrots are fork-tender. Taste and add more salt if needed. Prepare noodles: As the soup simmers, cook your favorite noodles according to package directions. Drain and divide the noodles between bowls. To serve: Ladle the soup over cooked noodles and serve with reserved minced scallion greens. Add a drizzle of chili oil on top if you like. Enjoy!
item.dc:creator: triedandtruerecipes

item.title: IRL shiny pokemon
item.description: Snowy owl spotted in Michigan. Article here (may be paywall): https://www.nytimes.com/2025/03/11/science/snowy-owl-orange-michigan-rusty.html. TL;DR - no one knows why it's orange. Some people think it could be a mutation, but the experts on mutations are like, "Nah". More likely explanation is that it was accidentally dyed somehow, possibly by airplane de-icer.
item.dc:creator: pangolinx

item.title: Facebook marketing scam breakdown
item.description:
this post was filled with comments of people saying they loved "Paul Richard" bags and AI images of them holding them up.
Paul is AI
in december of 2024, this website didn't exist
The current website looks like a legitimate retail website.
the AI portrait of "Paul Richard" is superficially similar but not even close to looking like the same person when viewed up close.
even at 70% off, these are 200+ dollar bags.
this is not the first Paul Richard website though, this scam has been ran multiple times. https://www.paul-richard.com/ was the previous URL and it is now dead. People pay money, never get an item and if they try to follow up, the website is gone and there's no way to contact the seller. Stay safe out there!
item.dc:creator: firegoat

item.title: Going, going, Gone.
item.description: A Cybertr, erm, WankPanzer has sunk in the harbor in Ventura, Calif. after its driver attempted to load a jet ski into the water. Musk previously claimed the WankPanzer could be used as a boat for a short period of time, allowing the vehicle to cross rivers.

item.title: Unstable Genius soiling the White House is working hard on a worldwide boycott of US goods
item.description: It's obvious that the demented Idiot in the WH only has to threaten tariffs to make this boycott happen, which is quickly gaining momentum. One of my favorite grocery shops here in Germany today started a clearance sale of everything American. Whiskeys, Trader Joe and Mike Mitchell stuff, everything dirt cheap with a sign saying it will not be re-stocked. So much winning, incredible!
item.dc:creator: Che07

item.title: Playing Fetch With the Harmonica Mortar
item.dc:creator: MayMyEnemiesLiveLong

item.title: Needless to say, the family dinner was awkward.
item.description: My siblings (much older than me) are very lucky that they have been able to stay in the same companies and move consistently upwards. That is not normal these days. At least in Europe you get things like benefits, public transportation (usually) and vacation days. I was astonished that my current job gave me 96 hours (12 days) of PTO a year as it was almost double my previous job and the other offer I received. They responded about needing to pay US & European taxes. “Well that’s only if I remain a US citizen, which I am seeing less and less reason to.”
item.dc:creator: Kairuf

item.description: Love the Irwin family and hope they continue to spread a universal love of all wildlife.

item.title: Don't let that sink in - the time to act gets shorter by the minute
item.dc:creator: mos6502c

item.title: I made a video of a teriyaki bottle going through an existential crisis
item.description:
Why would you make this poor teriyaki bottle go through an existential crisis? Good question. Basically I saw a creative contest for a trip to Japan that literally just said “make anything creative using this Teriyaki bottle”. Super vague requirements so naturally I made a video about the teriyaki bottle going through an existential crisis and he just wants to go back home to Japan to see his family.
Thought you guys might think it’s funny. Let me know what you think. Does it make sense? I was trying to go for the “early 2000s Chef Boyardee can comes to life and chases after little girl” vibe.
I made him a little hobo bindle with a chopstick, as one does when running away.
I made it into the top 6 so fingers crossed. Hopefully he gets his happy ending in Japan…wait no, I mean with his family. FP Edit: Appreciate you guys! I’m glad the story comes through. If anyone wants to help vote for my video I’d really appreciate it but no pressure: www.kikkomanteriyakithatchallenge.com/vote
item.dc:creator: ItsMoni
